rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF NEIL KEENEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/28/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Trustee has not yet filed her first account. A status date of 6/28/23 will be added to case PTR-21-304725. (2) It also appears that Trustee's bond is insufficient, if Conservator transferred over $2,000,000 to the trust in 2022. (3) A copy of the seller's closing statement must be filed. (4) Fees have not yet been reviewed in detail; fees will be considered with the trustee's accounting petition and any request for compensation in that proceeding. (5) The date of any order authorizing fees must be set forth in the accounting. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/16/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAUREEN E. CROWE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/3/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proof of service of the MANDATORY JUDICIAL COUNCIL form Notice of Hearing has been filed. [Probate Code Section 12201.] (2) The petition prayer still has not been supplemented to set forth the amount of additional time for estate administration being sought. (3) Numerous "status reports" by the attorney are NOT adequate. A petition must be either supplemented or amended by the Court-appointed fiduciary. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.3 and 7.103(c).] (4) Nothing appears to have been filed yet to address the apparent conflict of the attorney, who previously represented the petitioning beneficiaries, now representing the executor. If these issues are not addressed in time to provide courtesy copies of the EXECUTOR's verified supplement to her petition and a correct proof of service of the MANDATORY JUDICIAL COUNCIL form Notice of Hearing (which also provides the instructions for appearing remotely, as required by LRSF 14.1 D.6.), an appearance will be required on 7/3/23 for the Court to determine if this now very stale petition should be taken off calendar and a new status hearing set for the filing of the executor's first accounting for the two-year period beginning 6/5/19. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: The copy of the will should be filed again either by itself with a cover sheet or attached to the executor's supplement; the one attached to the initial Petition for Probate filed on 11/13/20 does not comport with CRC 2.104 and is nearly impossible to read online.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Counsel to report the court with any updates. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENIO S. AMADOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/28/23. (No earlier available date.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) No proof of service of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing has been filed. (4) Notice must include the instructions regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] The petition was reviewed cursorily online as a ONE-TIME courtesy; it appears that the petition does not fully comply with LRSF 14.35 J. It also appears to be missing a proper prayer. Deadline is 6/16/23 for delivering the proposed order and endorsed filed courtesy copies of the petition, proof of service, and any supplement to the petition to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHEPPARD M. LEVINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1226-1228 26th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94122)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The reported sale was made (offer/bid accepted and returned to Court for confirmation) on 02/16/2023, before notice of sale was first published on 02/24/2023, and before the 03/17/2023 deadline specified in the published notice for submitting offers/bids under Probate Code section 10304(a)(2), and thus failed to comply with section 10306(a) and LRSF 14.31B. The report of sale and petition had to be filed after the publication was completed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 1/6/2010. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set for 5/21/24. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STANLEY STEVEN GLAROS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/23/2023. The following procedural issues to be cleared seven days prior. 1. Petition to identify trust beneficiaries. Petition #8 states Alexis as the beneficiary; however, Notice filed 3/24/23 lists Alexis Stephanie Glaros and Stavros Wagner as the beneficiaries. 2. Need explanation regarding one-half of page 7 is left as blank. 3. Petition initially alleged that PC sec. 6124 presumption does not apply in anticipation of locating the original will. However, now alleges the will is lost. Petitioner needs to supplement Attachment 3f(3) alleging if PC sec. 6124 presumption applies now. If so, how the presumption can be revoked. 4. Proposed Order to include a copy of Lost will being offered to probate.
